title: Lithospermum barbigerum
taxonomy: Lithospermum barbigerum is a plant species classified in the family Boraginaceae, commonly known as the borage family. This family is part of the order Borales, which is a group of flowering plants. The Boraginaceae family includes a diverse range of species, primarily found in temperate and subtropical regions. The classification of Lithospermum barbigerum is further categorized into the genus Lithospermum, which contains approximately 100 species of flowering plants. The Boraginaceae family is known for its unique characteristics, such as the presence of alkaloids and other secondary metabolites in its species.

intro: Lithospermum barbigerum, also known as Gromwell or Lithospermum, is a perennial plant species belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae). It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it effective in treating various conditions such as wounds, skin issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Lithospermum barbigerum include lithospermoside, lithospermic acid, and other alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Lithospermum barbigerum include teas, tinctures, and ointments, which are used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
